revision.text:
[[ja:生駒山口神社]] {{Expand Japanese|生駒山口神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikomayamaguchi Shrine | native_name = 生駒山口神社 | image = 生駒山口神社２.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikomayamaguchi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.645831|135.695673}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Ikomayamaguchi Shrine'''|生駒山口神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|lt=Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|simple|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|lt=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 37 ** P155: {{ill|Hegurinoiwatoko Shrine|lt=Hegurinoiwatoko Shrine|ja|平群石床神社|qid=Q119929663|1=Hegurinoiwatoko Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q97220447|ltq=Q97220447|qid=Q97220447|1=Heguri Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Heguri, Nara|lt=Heguri|ar|هيغوري|arz|هيجورى|ceb|Heguri-chō|en|Heguri, Nara|eo|Heguri|es|Heguri (Nara)|fa|هگوری، نارا|fr|Heguri|hy|Հեգուրի|it|Heguri|ja|平群町|ko|헤구리정|ms|Heguri, Nara|my|ဟဲဂုရိမြို့၊ နာရ|pl|Heguri|ru|Хегури|simple|Heguri, Nara|sv|Heguri|tl|Heguri, Nara|tr|Heguri|uk|Хеґурі (Нара)|zh|平群町|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} * {{ill|Q907495|ltq=Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Heguri district|lt=Heguri district|ja|平群郡|zh|平群郡|qid=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.645831|135.695673}} == image (P18) == * 生駒山口神社２.jpg == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041122181046/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22713 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180696 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2150005003485 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県生駒郡平群町櫟原5-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:生駒山口神社]] {{wikidata link|Q115740129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Ikoma Yamaguchi Shrine | Image = [[File:Ikoma Yamaguchi Shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Ichihara 5-1 | ISO = JP-29 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 38 | Latitude seconds = 44.8 | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 41 | Longitude seconds = 44.8 | Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} | Map = Japan Nara }} '''Ikoma Yamaguchi Shrine''' (いこまやまぐちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Ichihara, {{ill|lt=Heguri Town|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as an {{ill|lt=Taisha|Q11438310|qid=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}}-ranked {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. [[File:Ikoma Yamaguchi Shrine.jpg|Thumbnail|In front of the worship hall]] === Overview === In the 'Engishiki,' it appears as “Ikoma Yamaguchi Shrine,” and it is stipulated that it is to receive {{ill|lt=heihaku|Q11481885|qid=Q11481885|1=Hanpei}} offerings at the imperial court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}. However, in the {{ill|lt=Specially Selected Register of Deity Names|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}, the enshrined deity is recorded as {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}.{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/64 |title=Specially Selected Register of Deity Names |access-date=2024-4-8}} The subordinate shrine on the right side of the main hall is Ōjinja (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}). The subordinate shrines on the left side of the main hall, from right to left, are: Sarutahiko Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}), Hachiman Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}), Kasuga Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}), Itsukushima Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}), Inari Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|qid=Q719665|1=Inari Ōkami}}), and Ebisu Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Kami|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}){{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=History of Heguri Town|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|page=639}}. Also, to the right as one faces the Itsukushima Shrine, there is a stone engraved with “{{ill|lt=Shōichi-i|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} Hakuichi Daimyōjin (しょういちいはくいちだいみょうじん),” which is enshrined. This stone enshrines the white fox of Inari, an agricultural deity, and is said to also bring blessings for eye diseases. Before the early modern period, {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined here, but due to the {{ill|lt=anti-Buddhist movement|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}}, Susanoo-no-Mikoto was enshrined in place of the strongly Buddhist-associated Gozu Tennō. The date of founding and origins are not clearly known, but it is popularly also called Taki-no-Miya, and it is said to have originally been located beside a waterfall to the southwest. It is thought to have been a guardian deity of the Ikoma mountain range and was one of the ten Yamaguchi shrines. === Cultural Properties === At Ikoma Yamaguchi Shrine, a ritual called “Ohakitsuki” has continued in which, one week before the autumn grand festival, the male and female deities of the shrine visit the homes of parishioners, and on the eve of the festival, known as the yomimiya,{{Cite kotobank|word=Yomimiya|accessdate=2022-09-22}} they return to the shrine. This ritual has been designated as a Nara Prefecture-designated Intangible {{ill|lt=Folk Cultural Property|Q24895900|qid=Q24895900|1=Folk Cultural Property}}.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www1.g-reiki.net/pref.nara/reiki_honbun/k401RG00000907.html |title=Nara Prefecture-Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties |access-date=2022-09-19 |publisher=Nara Prefecture}} === Access === About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Motoyamaguchi Station|Q6918516|qid=Q6918516|lt=Motoyamaguchi|1=Motosanjōguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Ikoma Line|Q900218|qid=Q900218|1=Ikoma Line}}. Parking available. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=List of shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} * {{ill|lt=List of Nara Prefecture-designated cultural properties|Q11445166|qid=Q11445166|1=List of Nara Prefecture-designated cultural properties}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikomayamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikomayamaguchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]]
